content: Just as broad-spectrum biological/chemical agents exert targeted, cascading effects on complex biological systems by interacting with existing molecular pathways, modern 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning LLM agents can be framed as 'cognitive effect agents' that exert targeted, adaptive changes to open-ended human cognitive and workflow systems by interacting with existing human memory structures, rather than only acting as task-completion tools. This re-framing unlocks new design principles for agent memory that prioritize adaptive effect over perfect recall.
